results.possibleIdentity: Stephen Vogel was a physiologist active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, based in Pasadena, Texas, near Houston. He was employed by the Boeing Company, where his work focused on human factors and space-related physiology, particularly during the era of the Apollo program. Vogel was a member of the non-profit organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), which fostered collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. Within the E.A.T. network, he identified his technical expertise in the field of "human function amplification," a term related to human-machine integration and the enhancement of human capabilities through technological means. His professional location in the Houston area suggests involvement with Boeing's contracted work for NASA's Manned Spacecraft Center.
